sections.text: This Act may be cited as the Appropriation Act, 1994. Short title

(1) On the the authority of a warrant issued by the President, Appropria­
there may expended from the general revenues of the Republic such lion
moneys, not exceeding in the aggrcgare six hundred and eighty-six
billion, eight hundred and five million, seven hundred and fifty-
seven thousand, one hundred and nineteen kwacha as may be
required for the services of the Republic during the financial year
ending on 31st December, 1994.
(2) The moneys referred to in subsection (I) shall be appropri­
ated to the services of the Republic spcci ficd in the second column
of the Schedule in amounts not exceeding the amounts indicated in
the third column of that Schedule.
(3) On the written authority of1hc Permanent Secretary, Minis­
try of Finance, the amounts shown under the sub-heads of the
estimates may be varied:
Provicted that-
the amounts appropriated to each Head shall not thereby be
(a )
altered; and
any of the amounts shown under Head 20 or 21 shall not
(b)
be varied.
Single copies of this Art may be obtained from the Governmenl Printer,
P.O. Box 30/3t>. JO/(}() Lu.,aka. Price: K/20.00
